title: Mark a task as done

story.i_want_to: mark a task as completed by its ID
story.so_that: I can track my progress and see what I've accomplished

rationale: Completing tasks is the core feedback loop of a todo app. Without it, the list only grows and becomes discouraging. Marking tasks done gives users a sense of accomplishment and keeps the list meaningful.

acceptance_criteria.description: Running `todo done 3` changes the status of task with id=3 from 'pending' to 'done'.
The completed_at field is set to the current ISO 8601 timestamp when a task is marked done.
The app prints 'Completed task {id}: {description}' to stdout.
If the task ID does not exist, the app prints 'Error: Task {id} not found.' to stderr and exits with code 1.
If the task is already done, the app prints 'Task {id} is already completed.' to stdout and exits with code 0.
Running `todo done` without an ID prints 'Error: Please provide a task ID. Usage: todo done <id>' to stderr and exits with code 1.
If the provided ID is not a positive integer (e.g., 'abc', '0', '-5', '3.5'), the app prints 'Error: Task ID must be a positive number.' to stderr and exits with code 1.
If more than one ID is provided (e.g., `todo done 1 2 3`), only the first ID is processed. Extra arguments are ignored.

test_cases.name: Mark pending task as done
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1, status='pending'
expected_results: completed_at is a valid ISO 8601 timestamp

test_cases.name: Complete non-existent task returns error
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1
expected_results: Raises ValueError or returns error indicating task not found

test_cases.name: Complete already-done task is idempotent
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1, status='done', completed_at='2026-03-25T10:00:00'
expected_results: Returns indication that task is already completed

test_cases.name: CLI done command end-to-end
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing pending task id=1

test_cases.name: Already-done task shows informational message
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1, status='done'

test_cases.name: Non-numeric ID shows error
steps: Run `python todo.py done abc` with HOME=tmp_dir
expected_results: stderr contains 'Error: Task ID must be a positive number.'

test_cases.name: Zero and negative IDs show error
expected_results: Both print 'Error: Task ID must be a positive number.' to stderr

test_cases.name: Missing ID shows usage hint
expected_results: stderr contains 'Error: Please provide a task ID.'

test_cases.name: Very large task ID returns not-found error gracefully
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1

technical_notes: Find task by iterating task list and matching id
ID from CLI args comes as string — convert to int with validation
Validate ID is positive integer before lookup (try int(), check > 0)
Update both status and completed_at fields
Save entire task list back to file after modification
All error messages to stderr, all success messages to stdout
Future enhancement: consider `todo undone <id>` to reopen tasks (out of scope for v1)
